title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence · Runtime Lens: Parser Differential
summary: A selector works under one parser but fails under another because omitted elements, foster parenting, broken nesting, and script or noscript state produce different trees. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parser differential increment in bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.
Handle script, style, template, comments, noscript, and raw-text elements without executing them or mistaking directives for visible content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Parser Differential, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison.

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-parser-differential — Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_html_parser_models_2(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"

# Evidence: scraper-parser-differential-5o0ifn
# Competency: scraper-parser-differential.
# Case 1494: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_parser_differential_5o0ifn(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_5o0ifn = "5o0ifn-nfi0o5-1494"
    assert evidence_variant_5o0ifn
    evidence_axes_5o0ifn = (
        "authorization5o0ifn",
        "url1494",
        "responsenfi0o5",
        "tree5o0ifn1494",
        "frontier14945o0ifn",
        "reportnfi0o51494",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_5o0ifn)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"

Keep the Parser Differential artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the parser differential defect evidence boundary for Parser Differential.

For repair the parser differential defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Parser Differential for bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Parser Differential: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 9497 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential.
steps.why: Retrieving Parser Differential through the concrete bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential, keep Parser Differential connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Parser Differential precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 9497 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Differential model · Theory Parser Differential, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Parser differential tests are unnecessary once the happy fixture passes.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: scraper-parser-differential-5o0ifn.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-parser-differential-5o0ifn(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_parser_differential_5o0ifn\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?tokenized != fixtures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?executed_scripts > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
